content: We can reframe agent-orchestrated answering ensembles as a microbial community of biological agents, where each AI answering agent functions like a specialized biological pathogen or enzyme that exerts power to produce a specific effect on the collective output, with the lead orchestrator agent coordinating their activity just as an ecosystem regulates collective microbial action to break down complex problems. This framing suggests that tuning answering ensemble diversity to match problem complexity, analogous to how soil microbial diversity matches ecosystem disturbance complexity, can improve overall ensemble accuracy.
